LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JULIE BALLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON (1)FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, AND (2)DEFENDANTS' DUTY TO COMPLY WITH CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS TO PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEE RETIREMENT SYSTEM AND GARY AMELIO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS GRANTED. THE SF CHARTER ESTABLISHES THE SF EMPLOYEE RETIREMENT SYSTEM. THE ADMINISTRATIVE CODE DOES NOT AUTHORIZE THE EMPLOYEE RETIREMENT SYSTEM TO AWARD SERVICE CREDIT FOR PERIODS OF UC RESIDENT/INTERN SERVICE AC SECTION 16:29-15.11 16.55-1. ESTOPPEL DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE THE EMPLOYEE RETIREMENT SYSTEM DID NOT POSSESS THE AUTHORITY TO AWARD THE SERVICE CREDITS FOR PERIODS OF TIME PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ELIGIBLE TO RECEIVE THEM. MEDINA V. BD OF RETIREMENT, LA CO. EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT ASSN. (2003) 112 CAL.APP.4TH 864. A CONTRACT ENTERED INTO WITHOUT THE REQUISITE CONSTITUTIONAL OR STATUTORY AUTHORITY IS VOID. SEE PAGE V. CITY OF MONTEBELLO (1980) 112 CAL.APP.3D 658. AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION BURDEN WAS SHIFTED, SHE RECEIVED THE CONTRIBUTIONS OWED & INTEREST AND THEY WERE SUBJECT TO TAXATION. PLAINTIFF RAISES NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THAT THE AMOUNTS WERE IN ERROR. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JULIE BALLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON (1)FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, AND (2)DEFENDANTS' DUTY TO COMPLY WITH CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATIONS TO PLAINTIFF.

APPEARANCES: Morris J. Baller, Esq. of Goldstein, Demchak, Baller, Borgen & Dardarian 510/763-9800 for pltf & moving party; Larry Hecimovich, Deputy City Atty 554-3933 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court denied plaintiff's motion for summary judgment. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEE RETIREMENT SYSTEM AND GARY AMELIO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.

APPEARANCES: Morris J. Baller, Esq. of Goldstein, Demchak, Baller, Borgen & Dardarian 510/763-9800 for pltf & opposing party; Larry Hecimovich, Deputy City Atty 554-3933 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court granted defendants motion for summary judgment. The SF Charter establishes the SF Employee Retirement System. The Administrative Code does not authorize the Employee Retirement System to award service credit for periods of UC Resident/Intern Service AC section 16:29-15.11 16.55-1. Estoppel does not apply because the Employee Retirement System did not possess the authority to award the service credits for periods of time plaintiff was not eligible to receive them. Medina v. Bd of Retirement, LA Co. Employees Retirement Assn. (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 864. A contract entered into without the requisite constitutional or statutory authority is void. See Page v. City of Montebello (1980) 112 Cal.App.3d 658. As to 2nd cause of action burden was shifted, she received the contributions owed & interest and they were subject to taxation. Plaintiff raises no triable issue of fact that the amounts were in error. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2011 Set For Trial On Feb-06-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jan-20-12 At 03:00 P.M. In Dept 504

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
